text:
In Slovakia, new parties with an anti-corruption and anti-politics agenda frequently enter the political scene and therefore ends the familiar pattern of alternating left and right coalition formats. Despite high degree of internal ideological uncertainty of the new entrants, their participation in the government did not lead to any considerable lengthening of the process of composing the cabinet or to the need for negotiation of a new coalition agreement. The ‘Ministerial Government Model’ has recently been stabilising and government coalitions are increasingly regulated by an agreement signed by party leaders on the distribution of ministries, internal coalition relations and the solution of potential deadlocks by means of a coalition council.

In Slovakia, the process of democratic institutionalization proceeded without much interruption; the effects of the financial and migration crises on domestic stability in Slovakia were slight and had an indirect influence on the level of democracy in the country. The COVID-19 pandemic turned out to be an exception as it gave prime minister Å'Hodora' an opportunity to regain his lost influence temporarily, therefore interrupting, though only momentarily, the trend of moving towards a more cooperative, ministerial mode of governance. Based on our data, the institutionalisation of rules and routines as part of the process of democratic institutionalisation progressed in Slovakia, while at the same time populist prime ministers continued to obstruct the effective application of these norms through acting out their characteristic individualistic and intolerant governing style, thus frequently breaking the coalition agreements regulating the relations within the ruling coalition and violating their underlying shared understandings.
explanation: The text is coherent political analysis but contains unusual diacritics (Å'Hodora) and some awkward phrasing typical of AI-generated or heavily paraphrased content. The mix of generic claims and stylized, formal language suggests a noticeable probability of AI authorship.
